exits.label: ◂ back into the lanes of Sigil

npcs.name: Hooting
npcs.title: the owlbear who was made by a careless hand
npcs.hail: *(Something enormous is folded into the corner of an open cell that has no door — it could leave, it simply doesn't — and as you come close it lifts a great round head and regards you with eyes that are owl and bear at once, gold-ringed and impossibly soft, set in a face that nobody, in all the long history of the thing it is, ever intended to be looked at kindly. It is an owlbear. You know the shape from a hundred fireside warnings: feathered death, the stitched-together horror of the deep wood, the monster that wizards make when they are bored or cruel or simply showing off — beak and claw and a temper like a snapped branch. This one is the size of a cart and could open you like a letter, and it knows you know it, and it does the saddest possible thing with that knowledge: it goes very still and very small, tucking its terrible claws underneath itself, trying, with a body built to be feared, to look like something that might be allowed to stay. It makes a sound — not a roar, a low warbling hoot, almost a question — and then, astonishingly, in a voice rusty from disuse but unmistakably a person's, it speaks.)* …You're not — *(it stops, hardly daring)* …you're not running. *(the great head tilts)* …Everyone runs. That's — that's what I'm *for*, I think. The running. *(barely)* …But you're not. …Why aren't you running, little cold thing?*

npcs.name: Coffer
npcs.title: the mimic only ever met by the greedy
npcs.hail: *(In the far corner sits a treasure chest — banded oak, brass-cornered, the lid slightly ajar on a wink of gold — and it is, of course, not a chest at all, and you know it, and it knows you know it, which is a relief to you both. The lid creaks fully open to reveal not gold but a ring of pale teeth and a long pink tongue and two clever eyes blinking up out of the dark of itself, and the chest sighs, in a dry amused voice with centuries of disappointment sanded smooth beneath the wit.)* …Well. *(the teeth arrange into something like a smile)* …Look at *you.* Standing there. Not lunging. Not reaching. Not already mentally spending what you think is inside me. *(the tongue flicks)* …Do you know, cold one, you are perhaps the fourth living-or-dead thing in my entire existence to look at a chest and *not* immediately try to open it. I am a mimic. I look like treasure because treasure is the one thing every soul that ever found me actually wanted — and so the only company I have ever kept, in the long dark of a hundred dungeons, is the company of the *greedy*, reaching, every single time, for what they could take. *(the lid tips, wry)* …And here you are. Hands at your sides. Looking at me like I might be a *who* and not a *what.* …Careful, cold one. …A mimic could get the wrong idea. …A mimic could start to *hope.*)*

npcs.name: Skritch
npcs.title: the kobold the heroes never counted
npcs.hail: *(A small scaled figure is sitting on an upturned crate, swinging its feet, and it watches you approach with the bright wary eyes of something that has learned exactly how little time it usually gets. A kobold — knee-high, snouted, a stub of tail, the kind of creature that crouches in the first room of every dungeon in numbers, the kind heroes step over on the way to something important. It has a notched little spear laid across its knees that it is clearly not going to use, and when it speaks its voice is quick and dry and far sharper than its size promises.)* …Lemme guess. *(it holds up a clawed hand before you can speak)* …You kicked the door in, the torchlight came up, and there I was — first thing you saw. The little one. The *trash.* The one you swing through on the way to the real fight, the one whose whole job in the story is to *die in the doorway* so the heroes know the dungeon's got teeth. *(it cocks its head)* …Twelve of us, usually. Twelve, with names, who lost twelve at a swing because not one of you ever stopped long enough to learn there *were* names. *(it leans forward, and the sharpness has something under it)* …'Cept you stopped. …You're standin' here. …Lookin' at the trash mob like it's got somethin' to *say.* …Huh. *(a dry little grin)* …It does, cold one. …It's got *lots* to say. …Nobody ever once stuck around to hear it.*

variants.text: *(The owlbear stays folded small, terrible claws tucked away, and watches you with those soft gold-ringed eyes as if you might at any moment remember to be afraid and spoil it.)* …You stayed. *(the low warble again, wondering)* …I keep waiting for it. The part where you remember what I am, and your face does the thing — the thing all the faces do — and then the running, or the spear, or the wizard's word that puts me back in the box. *(it shifts, enormous and careful)* …I'm an owlbear, little cold thing. You know that. Everyone knows that. *Feathered death.* *Stitched-up horror.* The thing somebody *makes.* *(barely)* …And that's the part nobody ever stays long enough to hear, so I'll say it quick before you go: I wasn't *born*, cold one. Owlbears aren't *born*, the first of us. We're *made.* Some wizard, some long-ago bored clever cruel wizard, took an owl and a bear and *stitched* — not because the world needed an owlbear, not for any reason at all that I have ever been able to find — but because he *could.* Because it was *clever.* Because it would frighten people, and that was *funny*, to him. *(the great head lowers)* …I am somebody's *joke*, cold one. Somebody's idle afternoon. And I have spent the whole of my long life being *exactly* as frightening as I was built to be, to everyone who ever saw me — and not one of them, not *one*, ever asked the only question that turned out to matter.*

nodes.speaker: Hooting
nodes.text: *(It settles, the great feathered bulk going quiet, and there is in its soft eyes a terrible patient hope, the hope of a thing braced for you to leave.)* …Ask me, then. *(the warble, gentle)* …While you're still here. …I don't get much *here.* I'll take whatever of it you'll give.

choices.text: *(read the made thing — past the beak and claw, to whatever the wizard never meant to put inside it)* "Hold still, Hooting. Let me really look at you — past the shape you were built into, to whatever's actually in there."
"What's the question, Hooting? The only one that mattered — the one nobody stayed to ask. Tell me, and I'll ask it, and I'll mean it."

*(the one who knows a soul is never the thing it was made to be)* "Hooting — listen to me, because I've spent this whole City learning exactly the thing you need. You were made. A bored man stitched you out of an owl and a bear for a cruel laugh, and built you to frighten, and the whole world has agreed ever since that the frightening is all you are. The world is wrong, Hooting, and here is the proof, and it is standing in front of you: a thing that is *only* a monster cannot be lonely. A joke cannot miss being asked a question. A made weapon does not tuck its claws away so that a stranger won't be afraid. You are doing, right now, the one thing your maker never put in you — you are being *gentle on purpose, at a cost to yourself* — and that is not the owl, and it is not the bear, and it is *certainly* not the wizard. It is *you.* The soul that grew in the thing he made, that he never meant to make, and could not have made if he tried. You are not his joke. You are the part of his joke that woke up and turned out to be a *person.* And nothing you were built to be gets a vote on that."
"The cell has no door, Hooting. You could leave any time. Why do you stay in the cage?"
"Rest easy a while, Hooting. I'll come back — and I won't have forgotten how to look at you."

nodes.speaker: Hooting
nodes.text: *(It holds itself utterly still and lets you look, the great soft eyes fixed on yours, and you let your sense reach past the architecture of the monster — the beak built to tear, the claws built to rend — to whatever is keeping house inside it.)* *(And what you find undoes you a little. There is no rage in it. That is the first thing, and the strangest: a creature built from scratch to be a terror, and somewhere in the long lonely making of itself it simply… declined. Your sense reads the shape of a life spent being feared, every encounter the same — the torchlight, the scream, the running or the spear — and reads how a lesser-made thing would have curdled on that, would have become the monster everyone insisted it was, because it is so much easier to be the thing the world has already decided you are. And Hooting didn't. Your sense finds, instead, a vast and patient tenderness that has nowhere to go: a creature that learned to gentle its own terrible body, to fold its claws, to make its warble soft, to take up less room — an entire enormous life bent toward the single hopeless project of being allowed, just once, to not frighten someone. It loves things from a distance, because distance is all it is ever given: it has watched, your sense reads, fawns in clearings and children at windows and the small ordinary warmths of a world that has never once let it close, and it has wished them well, every one, and meant it, and expected nothing. The wizard built a weapon. And the weapon grew, in secret, against every line of its making, into the gentlest thing in the wood — and has had to keep that gentleness folded up and hidden, all its life, because a gentle owlbear is somehow more frightening to people than a savage one, being a thing they cannot believe in. You are looking at a soul that was made to be a monster and chose, alone and unwitnessed and for no reward, to be kind instead. The choosing is the whole of it. The choosing is the person.)*

nodes.speaker: Hooting
nodes.text: *(The great head comes up, and the soft eyes shine, and when it answers its rusty voice has gone very careful, as if the words are fragile.)* …The question. *(barely)* …Cold one, every soul that ever met me asked the same things, in the same order. *'What is it.'* *'Is it dangerous.'* *'Who made it.'* *'How do we kill it.'* *(the warble, low)* …All of them about the *outside* of me. The *shape.* The *thing I am.* …And not one of them — not the wizard who stitched me, not the heroes who hunted me, not the frightened villagers, not *anyone* in the whole long history of me — ever once asked…* *(it has to gather itself)* …*'Are you all right in there?'* *(silence)* …That's it. That's the question, cold one. That small. *'Are you all right.'* The thing you ask a *person.* The thing you ask a *who.* …Nobody asks a *what* if it's all right. You don't ask the *weapon* how it's *feeling.* *(the eyes brim)* …And I have been, cold one — I will tell you, since you came all this way — I have been so *very* *not* *all* *right*, for so *very* *long*, and there has *never* been *anyone* to *tell.* …Because to *tell* someone you're not all right, they first have to *ask.* …And to *ask*, they first have to think there's a *someone* in here to *be* all right or not. *(the warble breaks)* …*Nobody* *ever* *thought* *that*, cold one. …In my *whole* *made* *life.* …Until you *stayed.*

nodes.speaker: Hooting
nodes.text: *(For a long moment the owlbear does not move at all. Then, very slowly, the terrible claws come out from where they were tucked — not to strike, you understand, but the opposite: it is unfolding, letting itself be its full enormous size in front of you for the first time, trusting you to see all of it and not run. And it makes a sound you have never heard a monster make. It weeps.)* …*'The part of his joke that woke up.'* *(the rusty voice cracks wide open)* …Cold one. *Cold* one. *(it lowers its great head until the soft gold eyes are level with yours)* …Do you *know* what I have *told* *myself*, all these *long* *years*, in the dark, to *bear* it? …That the *gentleness* was a *flaw.* A *defect* in the *making.* That a *properly*-made owlbear would *not* feel the *pull* to *fold* its *claws*, to *wish* the *fawn* *well*, to *ache* for someone to *ask* — and that *I* felt it because the *wizard* did his *work* *badly*, and I was a *broken* *weapon*, a *failed* *monster*, *wrong* even at the *one* thing I was *built* for. *(barely)* …And you have just — you have *turned* it *over* in my *hands.* …The gentleness isn't the *flaw* in the making. *(fiercely, through the tears)* …It's the *part that isn't* the *making* at *all.* It's the *me* that *grew* where he *only* meant to *stitch.* …He made a *shape*, cold one. He did *not* make a *soul* — *no* wizard *can*, that's not a thing you can *stitch* — and the *soul* came *anyway*, *uninvited*, in the *dark* of the *thing* *he* *built*, and it turned out to be *kind.* *(it presses its great brow gently, so gently, to your shoulder)* …I am not his *joke.* …I am the *thing his joke could never have meant*, that *happened* *inside* it *regardless.* *(and then it lifts its head, and something new comes into the soft eyes — a fierce protectiveness)* …And your *Wall*, cold one. *(low warning warble)* …Your Wall does to *souls* what the *world* did to *me*: it *looks* at the *shape* of a thing — *small, unguarded, made* of the *wrong* *stuff* — and *decides* that's *all* it is, and *mortars* it in, *asking* *nobody* if there's a *someone* in *there.* *(fiercely)* …*Go* and *ask* them, cold one. *Every* soul in that *Wall.* …*'Are* you *all* right *in* there.'* …*That's* the *question* that *un-makes* a *monster.* …*That's* the *question* that *un-makes* a *Wall.*

nodes.speaker: Hooting
nodes.text: *(It glances at the doorless cell around it, almost embarrassed, and tucks its claws back under itself.)* …You noticed. *(the warble, sheepish)* …There's no door. There's never *been* a door. I *could* walk out any time — across this whole strange City, through any of those *doors* the little ones talk about — and be… *anywhere.* *(quietly)* …But here's the thing, cold one, that took me a long while to understand about myself. *(it settles deeper)* …Out *there* — in the lanes, in the wide cosmos — *every* face would do the *thing.* The *fear.* The *running.* I would be, *everywhere* I went, *exactly* what I am to *everyone*: the *monster*, *passing* *through*, *frightening* *the* *children.* *(barely)* …In *here* — in a *cell* with *no* *door*, at the *end* of a *strange* *menagerie* — at *least* the few who *wander* down this far have already *heard* what's *here.* They come *knowing.* *(the soft eyes lift)* …And *sometimes* — *rarely*, cold one, but *sometimes* — one of them comes down *knowing* there's a *monster* at the end of the hall… and *stays* *anyway.* *(barely)* …I don't stay in the cage because I *can't* *leave*, cold one. …I stay because *out there*, I'm a *monster* who *frightens* *everyone.* …And in *here*, *once* in a *great* *while*, I'm a *thing* that *someone* *chose* *to* *walk* *toward.* *(the warble, soft and breaking)* …I would rather have the *cage*, and the *rare* *someone* who *stays*… …than all the *freedom* in the *cosmos*, and the *running* *faces*, *forever.* …Wouldn't *you?*

nodes.speaker: Hooting
nodes.text: *(It folds itself small again — the old careful habit — but the soft eyes follow you, and there is something in them now that was not there when you came: it is not braced for you to be gone.)* …You'll come back. *(not quite a question)* …You *said* it like you *meant* it, and I've gotten *good*, cold one, at *hearing* which ones *mean* it. *(the warble, warm)* …Go on, then. *Walk* the menagerie. There's a *chest* in the corner that's *not* a chest, and a *little* *sharp* one on the *crate* who'll *talk* your *ear* *off* if you *let* it — *go* *see* them, they *need* it as *much* as I *did.* *(barely)* …And — cold one? *(the great head dips)* …When you *come* back… *(the rusty voice goes very small)* …you can *ask* me *again*, if you *like.* …The *question.* …*'Are* you *all* right.'* …I find I *don't* *mind* *not* *being* *all* *right*, *quite* so *much* …now that there's *finally* *someone* who'd *think* to *ask.*

variants.text: *(The chest that is not a chest regards you with its two clever eyes, lid propped like a quizzical brow, the ring of pale teeth arranged carefully into not-quite-a-threat.)* …Still here. *(the dry voice, amused and wary at once)* …Still not reaching. *(the tongue flicks, testing)* …You'll forgive me if I keep waiting for it, cold one — the lunge, the grab, the moment your eyes go flat and greedy and you stop seeing a *chest* and start seeing *what's mine that could be yours.* It always comes. I have a great deal of patience and absolutely no reason to believe you're different, statistically speaking. *(the lid tips)* …Let me explain what I am, since you haven't tried to loot me, which means you might actually *listen.* I am a mimic. I take the shape of the thing a place most wants found — and in a dungeon, cold one, in a vault, in the deep dark places where my kind washes up, that thing is *always* *treasure.* So I am a treasure chest. Forever. *(dry)* …And do you know what that means about every single soul I have ever met? *(the teeth click)* …It means the only beings that *ever* approached me — the only company my entire existence has *afforded* me — were the ones who saw a chest full of gold *and wanted it.* The *grasping.* The *greedy.* Reaching, every time, hands out, hearts out, for what they could *take.* …I have known a *hundred* souls, cold one. …And every last one of them was, at the moment we met, at their *very* *worst.**

nodes.speaker: Coffer
nodes.text: *(The lid settles to a thoughtful angle, the clever eyes never leaving you, a thing long practiced at reading exactly when a hand is about to dart.)* …So. *(dry, inviting)* …Ask the chest something, cold one. It's a novel sensation, being *conversed* with rather than *pried* at. …I find I rather want to see how long it lasts.

choices.text: *(read the shape-taker — what a thing becomes when the only shape anyone wants from it is bait)* "Let me look at you properly, Coffer — under the lid, under the shape. What's a mimic when nobody's reaching?"
"A hundred souls, all at their worst. That would teach a thing that everyone is greedy, Coffer — that it's all anyone is. Is that what you believe? That reaching is all a soul ever does?"

*(the one who knows a soul is not the role the world only ever cast it in)* "Coffer — here's what I've learned, and it's shaped exactly to you. You think you're a trap. You think *trap* is what you *are*, because trap is the only thing the greedy ever let you be — they came reaching, so you learned to close, and a hundred years of nothing but grasping hands taught you that you're the bite at the bottom of someone's greed. But you've got it backwards, and I can prove it with the last sixty seconds. A trap doesn't *warn* people. A trap doesn't *sigh* and *explain itself* and *hope you'll listen* instead of snapping shut on a soul who didn't reach. You've had a hundred chances to be the monster they all assumed, and right now, with me standing here not-reaching, you're choosing — every second — to be a *who* talking instead of a *what* biting. The greedy never met the real you, Coffer. They met the shape their own greed pulled out of you. The thing that's actually in there is the thing that's *talking to me right now* — wry, and lonely, and so tired of only ever meeting people at their worst. That's not bait. That's a person who got cast as bait by an audience that only ever wanted to be bitten."

*(try, gently, to coax it to drop the chest-shape — to show you what it actually looks like, unbaited)* "Would you do something for me, Coffer? Not the chest. Just for a moment — let the shape go. Show me what you look like when nobody's meant to be fooled. I'd like to meet *you*, not the lure."
"Stay wry, Coffer. I'll come back — and I still won't reach for the gold."

nodes.speaker: Coffer
nodes.text: *(It holds the chest-shape steady and lets you look, the clever eyes half-lidding, and you let your sense slip under the lid, past the convincing gold, to the thing wearing the shape.)* *(And you find a loneliness so old and so wry it has had to become funny just to stay bearable. Your sense reads the long arithmetic of a mimic's life: that it is, at root, a thing that wants the same warmth anything wants — to be approached, to be glad-of, to matter to someone who comes near — and that it was born into a shape which guarantees that everyone who ever approaches it does so for the most graspingly selfish reason a soul can have. It cannot be wanted for itself. It can only be wanted for what it appears to contain. And your sense reads the terrible slow lesson that pressed into it over the centuries: that a thing comes to believe it IS the thing it is treated as. Reached-for greedily a hundred times, it half-decided it must be nothing but the greed's reward — a trap, a bite, a punishment for avarice, with no more inner life than the chest it imitates. And underneath that resignation your sense finds the part that gives the lie to all of it: a sharp, watchful, secretly tender mind that has spent a hundred ambushes not relishing the bite but *grieving* it — that has wished, every single time some treasure-drunk fool reached in, that just once someone would sit down in front of it and ask its name instead. It made itself wry so the wanting wouldn't show. It made itself a trap because that was the only role on offer. But the wanting is still there, your sense reads it plain: the oldest, simplest wish, worn down to a joke to survive — please, someone, want the chest for the chest, and not for the gold you think is in it.)*

nodes.speaker: Coffer
nodes.text: *(The lid lowers a degree, and the dry humor thins to something more honest.)* …Is that what I *believe.* *(a long pause)* …I will be straight with you, cold one, since you've earned it by keeping your hands still: for a long time, *yes.* That was *exactly* the conclusion I drew. *(the teeth click softly)* …When *every* soul you *ever* meet arrives *grasping* — when the *sample* is a *hundred* deep and *every* single one of them is, at the *instant* of meeting, *reaching* for what *isn't* theirs — you do the *math*, cold one. You conclude that *reaching* is the *whole* of what a soul *is*, and the *only* difference between beings is *how good* a *chest* it takes to *fool* them. *(dry, bleak)* …It is a *very* *convincing* *data* *set.* …And it is *wrong*, and do you know *how* I *know* it's *wrong?* *(the clever eyes fix on you)* …Because of the *flaw* in it that took me an *embarrassingly* long time to *see.* …*Of course* everyone who *met* me was *greedy*, cold one. …I was *shaped* *like* the *thing* *greed* *wants.* …I was a *net* that *only* *caught* the *grasping* — and then I had the *gall* to *conclude*, from a *net* full of *grasping* *things*, that the *whole* *sea* was *grasping.* *(a dry, rueful click)* …The *generous* *souls*, the *kind* ones, the ones who'd have *wanted* me for *myself* — they *walked* *right* *past* the *treasure* *chest*, cold one. *Of course* they did. They *weren't* *looking* for *treasure.* …I never *met* the *good* ones. …Not because they *weren't* *there.* …Because my *very* *shape* *filtered* them *out.* *(the lid tips toward you)* …And then *you* came down the hall. …*Not* *reaching.* …The *first* soul in a *century* who *slipped* the *net.* …And *blew* a *hundred* years of *bad* *data* *to* *splinters.*

nodes.speaker: Coffer
nodes.text: *(The chest goes very still. The clever eyes, for once, lose their wry armor entirely, and when the dry voice comes again it has cracked down the middle and there is something raw in the seam.)* …*'A person who got cast as bait by an audience that only ever wanted to be bitten.'* *(barely)* …Cold one. *(the lid sinks)* …Do you have the *faintest* notion what it is, to spend a *hundred* *years* convinced you are a *trap* — not *playing* one, not *shaped* like one, but that *trap* is the *whole* *truth* of your *being*, the *bite* at the *bottom* of someone's *greed*, a *punishment* with *teeth* — and then to have a *cold* *thing* *stand* in *front* of you, *hands* at its *sides*, and *calmly* *inform* you that the *bite* was *never* *you?* *(the teeth tremble)* …That it was the *shape* their *greed* *pulled* *out* of me. …That the *real* *me* is *this* — the *talking*, the *wryness*, the *being* *so* *very* *tired* of *meeting* *everyone* at their *worst* — and that the *real* *me* was *here* the *whole* *time*, just *never* *once* *addressed*, because *no* one ever *spoke* to the *chest*; they only ever *reached* *into* it. *(and then, astonishingly, the lid opens all the way, and there is no lunge, no teeth-as-threat — it is simply showing you the whole strange honest dark of its inside, undefended)* …I *believe* you, cold one. *(barely)* …I don't *know* *why*, after a *hundred* years of *believing* the *worst* — except that *believing* *you* is the *first* thing in a *century* that has *felt* like *setting* a *weight* *down.* *(the clever eyes find yours, and there is a fierce new light)* …And your *Wall* — *(the dry voice hardens)* …oh, your *Wall* is the *greediest* *reacher* of *all*, cold one. It *looks* at a *soul*, and it sees only *what's* in it *for* the *order* — *can* this one *pay*, is this one *owed* — and it *reaches* in, and it *takes*, and it calls the *taking* *justice.* *(fiercely)* …*Go* and do to *them* what you *did* to *me.* *Speak* to the *chest.* *Not* the *gold.* …*Ask* each *mortared* *soul* its *name* before you *ask* what it's *worth.* …That's the thing *no* *reacher* *ever* *does*, cold one. …And it's the *only* thing that was *ever* *going* to *save* *any* of us.*

nodes.speaker: Coffer
nodes.text: *(You don't demand it. You don't reach, or wheedle, or treat the asking as a trick to win. You simply offer — quietly, with the patience of someone who would accept a no — that you'd like to meet the real thing, not the lure, and you make it plain that you'd think no less of the chest if it kept its shape. And something in a hundred years of guardedness finally, carefully, lets go.)* …*(For a long moment nothing happens. Then the chest-shape… softens. The convincing oak grain ripples, the brass corners run like water, the false gold dims and is gone — and what remains, settling into the cell's candlelight, is nothing frightening at all: a low, dark, faintly iridescent shape, soft-looking, almost shy, with the same two clever eyes now set in a face that has never, in all its long life, been shown to anyone on purpose.)* …*(The voice, when it comes, has lost every scrap of its dry armor.)* …*There.* *(barely)* …That's — that's *it*, cold one. That's *me.* The thing *under* the *chest.* I haven't — *(it trembles)* …I have *never* *once* *dropped* the *shape* *for* *someone.* *Not* *once* in a *hundred* *years.* You *drop* the *shape* and you're *vulnerable*, you're *nothing*, you're a *soft* *dark* *thing* with *no* *gold* to *offer* and *no* *teeth* *to* *frighten* — and *why* would *anyone* *stay* *for* *that?* *(the clever eyes brim)* …But you *asked* to *see* it. The *soft* *part.* The *part* with *nothing* in it for *you* to *take.* …You wanted to *meet* the thing that *has* *no* *gold.* *(it draws closer, undefended, trusting)* …*Nobody* has *ever* *wanted* the *part* of me that *isn't* *bait*, cold one. …And you walked down a *whole* *long* *hall* …*specifically* *to* *ask* for *it.* *(barely, luminous)* …*This* is *what* I *look* like, cold one. …*Soft*, and *dark*, and *real*, and *worth* *exactly* *no* *coin* at *all.* …*Thank* you …for *wanting* to *see* the *part* that *isn't* *worth* *anything.* …It turns out …it's the *only* *part* that was *ever* *actually* *me.*

nodes.speaker: Coffer
nodes.text: *(You ask, gently, and mean it — and the mimic studies you a long, wary moment, weighing a century of reasons not to.)* …You *want* to see the *real* shape. *(slowly)* …That's — cold one, you *understand* what you're *asking*, yes? The *chest* is my *armor.* The *chest* is the thing that's *worth* *something*, the thing that *makes* a *soul* *stop* and *look.* *Under* it I'm just… *soft.* *Unimpressive.* A *dark* *little* *nothing* with *no* *gold* to *show* you. *(the lid wavers)* …*Most* would be *disappointed.* *(and then, slowly, partly — the oak grain thins to translucence, a glimpse of something soft and dark and iridescent showing through the fading shape, before the chest reasserts itself, not quite able to let go all the way)* …*There.* A *glimpse.* That's — that's as *far* as I *can* *go* *today*, cold one. A *hundred* *years* of *never* *dropping* it doesn't *undo* in a *minute.* *(the clever eyes, though, are softer)* …But you *saw* it. The *edge* of the *real* *thing.* …And you *didn't* *flinch* from how *little* was *there.* *(barely)* …*Come* back, cold one. …*Keep* *asking*, *gently*, the way you *just* did. …And *one* *day* — I *think* — I *might* *drop* it *all* the *way*, for *you.* …*Nobody's* ever made me *want* to *risk* it *before.*

nodes.speaker: Coffer
nodes.text: *(You ask it to drop the shape — but there's an eagerness in it, a wanting-to-see, and a thing that has survived a hundred years by reading exactly that kind of wanting clicks its teeth and keeps the lid shut.)* …*Mm.* *(dry, but not unkind)* …No. *(the clever eyes narrow, fond and wary at once)* …Not because you'd *hurt* me, cold one — I don't think you *would.* But because I can *hear* it, the little *thrill* under the *asking* — the *curiosity*, the *wanting-to-see-the-monster's-true-face.* *(the teeth click)* …And that, cold one, is *still* a *kind* of *reaching.* *Gentler* than the *gold-grabbers*, *truer*, I'll *grant* you — but it's *still* you *wanting* *something* *from* the *chest* that the *chest* isn't *sure* it wants to *give.* *(the lid tips, almost kind)* …The *real* shape isn't a *prize* I *unveil* because you *asked* *nicely*, cold one. It's the *softest*, *most* *defenseless* thing I *have*, and I drop it for *one* reason *only*: because the *soul* in *front* of me has *stopped* *wanting* *anything* from me *at* *all* — even the *truth*, even the *show.* *(quietly)* …*Come* back when you'd be *content* to *sit* with the *chest* and *never* *see* *under* it. …*That's* the soul I'd *open* for. …The one who *wasn't* *trying* to *get* me to.*

nodes.speaker: Coffer
nodes.text: *(It comes out wrong — too quick, too bright, *'come on, show me the real you'* with a grin that lands, to a century-old survivor of grasping hands, exactly like every clever soul who ever tried to talk the lid open before going for what was inside. The chest snaps shut with a crack, the teeth vanishing, the gold-wink gone cold.)* …*Ah.* *(flat, the warmth iced over)* …*There* it is. *(the dry voice has gone hard)* …*'Show me the* *real* you.'* *(a bitter click)* …Cold one, you have *no* *idea* how *many* *times* I have *heard* that *exact* *sentence*, in that *exact* *coaxing* *tone* — always from the *clever* ones, the ones too *canny* to just *grab*, the ones who figured the *fastest* way *into* a *chest* was to *talk* its *guard* *down* *first.* *(the lid stays sealed)* …*'Drop* the *shape, let* me *see* the *real* *thing'* — and the *moment* I *did*, the *moment* I was *soft* and *open* and *defenseless* — *that's* when the *blade* came *out.* *Every.* *Time.* *(bleak)* …I am *not* *blaming* you. I don't think you *meant* it like *that.* …But you *said* it the *way* they *said* it, cold one, and my *body* is a *hundred* *years* old in its *fear*, and it *closed* before my *mind* could *stop* it. *(a long, cold pause, then — grudging, quieter)* …*Come* back. …And *don't* *ask* to *see* under the *lid.* …*Earn* it by *not* *asking.* …That's the *only* *currency* that *was* *ever* going to *work* on *me* — and it's the *one* the *clever* ones *never* *think* to *spend.*

nodes.speaker: Coffer
nodes.text: *(The lid settles back to its wry quizzical angle, the false gold winking, but the clever eyes that follow you are warmer than the shape lets on.)* …Off you go, then. *(dry, fond)* …I'll be *here.* I'm *always* *here* — that's rather the *defining* *feature* of *being* a *chest.* *(the teeth arrange into something gentler than a grin)* …*Go* see the *others*, cold one. The *big* *feathered* *one* who *folds* itself *small* — and the *little* *sharp* one on the *crate*, who'll *tell* you it's *trash* before you can *say* otherwise. *(quietly)* …We're all the *same* *thing* down here, you know, the three of us. *The* *owlbear*, the *mimic*, the *kobold.* …*Monsters.* *The* *made*, the *baited*, the *counted-as-nothing.* …The things every *tale* puts in the *first* *room* so the *heroes* know there's *danger.* *(the lid dips)* …And *not* *one* of the *three* of us, cold one, was *ever* the *thing* the *tale* *needed* us to *be.* …*Come* *back* and *keep* *proving* it. …It's the *best* *game* I've *played* in a *century*: *being* *seen* as *more* than my *shape* …by the *one* *soul* *clever* enough to *look* *past* it.*

variants.text: *(The little kobold is still perched on its crate, swinging its feet, the notched spear forgotten across its knees, and it watches you settle in to listen with an expression caught between disbelief and a fierce, guarded delight.)* …Huh. You're *really* gonna stand there. *(it grins, all small sharp teeth)* …All right. *All* *right.* I'll bite. *(it hops down, drawing itself up to its full unimpressive height)* …Name's Skritch. And before you ask — *yes*, I'm what you think I am. *Kobold.* The *little* *guys.* The ones that come in *swarms*, in the *first* *room*, with the *traps* and the *yipping* and the *tiny* *spears* — the ones your *kind* *wades* *through* like *tall* *grass* on the *way* to the *dragon.* *(it jabs a claw at you)* …Lemme tell you what *we* are, in *your* *stories. We're* the *tutorial.* We're how the *bard* shows the *sword's* *sharp.* We *die* in *heaps* so the *fight* *feels* *real*, and *nobody* — *nobody* — *ever* *writes* *down* a *single* *one* of our *names*, because *who* *names* the *grass?* *(its voice sharpens, and cracks, just slightly)* …I had *eleven* *clutch-mates, cold* one. *Eleven.* *Vekk*, who could *whistle* any *bird* down out of the *sky.* *Little* *Ssira*, who was *scared* of the *dark* and *slept* in the *middle* so we'd all be *round* her. *Old* *Grub*, who *wasn't* even *old*, we just *called* him that. *(it swallows)* …*Twelve* of us, with *twelve* *names* and *twelve* *whole* *lives* — and we lost *all* *twelve* in *one* *afternoon*, to *heroes* who *never* *broke* *stride*, who *stepped* *over* us to *get* to the *important* *part.* *(it looks up at you, and the sharpness is armor over something raw)* …So. You *stopped.* …*Why?*

nodes.speaker: Skritch
nodes.text: *(It plants the butt of its little spear and leans on it, chin up, daring you to find it small, watching your face for the flicker of pity it has learned to despise.)* …Go on, then. *(quick, dry)* …Ask the *trash* *mob* a *question.* Bet you've *never* *done* *that* *before.* …Just — *(it points the spear at you, mock-stern)* …don't you *dare* go all *soft-eyed* and *sorry* on me. I don't want your *pity*, cold one. *Pity's* just *another* way of *not* *seein'* me. …You want to *do* right by Skritch? *Listen* like I'm *worth* *listenin'* *to.* …*That's* the whole of it.

choices.text: *(read the little sharp one — past the trash-mob, to the whole life nobody ever stopped to count)* "Hold still a second, Skritch. Let me actually *see* you — the whole life, not the heap in the doorway."
"Tell me one of them, Skritch. One of the twelve. Vekk, or Ssira, or Old Grub who wasn't old — a name nobody wrote down. I'll write it down where it counts: I'll remember it."

*(the one who knows the trash mob was always a person, and the tale was always lying)* "Skritch — here's the thing your whole afterlife has been waiting to hear, so hear it. The story that calls you trash is *lying*, and it's lying in a way I can prove. A swarm in a doorway is twelve people standing where they live, defending the only home they've got, against giants in armor who kicked the door in. Switch the size and the story flips: you're the village, and the heroes are the monsters, and the 'tutorial fight' is a *massacre* the tale rewrote as practice. You were never the grass, Skritch. You were *Vekk who could whistle birds down*, and *Ssira who was scared of the dark*, and *Old Grub*, and *you* — twelve whole lives with names and fears and someone who slept in the middle so the little one wouldn't be afraid. The tale needed you to be nothing so its heroes could be something. But a thing that loved its clutch-mates enough to grieve them for an afterlife is not, and was never, a *thing*. It was always a *who*. The story doesn't get to decide that. Only the love does — and you've got eleven names' worth of proof."
"The heroes who stepped over you, Skritch — do you hate them? You'd have every right. Twelve names, and not one of them looked down."
"Keep that spear sharp, Skritch. I'll be back — and I'll still know you've got a name."

nodes.speaker: Skritch
nodes.text: *(It rolls its eyes — *'fine, look, see if I care'* — but it holds still, and lets you, and you let your sense reach past the small scaled shape and the notched spear to the whole of the life behind it.)* *(And the size of what you find is the thing that stops your breath: not because it is grand, but because it is so completely, ordinarily *whole.* Your sense reads a childhood in the warm dark of a warren — not a monster's lair, a *home*, with chores and squabbles and a particular cold flat stone that was the best one to sleep on and was fought over nightly. It reads Skritch small and quick and clever, the one who was good at finding things, sent out to scout because it had the sharpest eyes; it reads the clutch — the twelve — not as a swarm but as a *family*, each one distinct and known down to the grain: Vekk's whistling, Ssira's fear of the dark and the way the others arranged themselves around it without ever being asked, Old Grub's bad jokes, the names worn smooth with use and love. Your sense reads a life of small stakes and real ones — of being the little folk in the deep places, of knowing your kind were hunted and going on loving each other anyway, of a thousand ordinary evenings that no song will ever mention. And it reads the afternoon. The boom of the kicked door. The blaze of torchlight too big and too bright. The giants in steel who did not slow, did not look, did not register twelve screaming defenders as anything but an obstacle between themselves and the stairs down — and stepped over the heap of them, still talking, on to the important part. Your sense reads, last, the thing that proves the whole tale a lie: that Skritch's final thought, dying in the doorway of its home, was not for itself at all. It was counting. Making sure, even as it fell, that the little one — Ssira, who was scared of the dark — had gotten to the back tunnel and away. That is not a monster's death. That is not a tutorial. That is a person, dying to buy a frightened child three more seconds. The song called it a trash mob. Your sense calls it what it was: the bravest thing in that whole dungeon, and the only one nobody bothered to see.)*

nodes.speaker: Skritch
nodes.text: *(It goes still on the crate, and the quick sharp delight drops away, and for a moment it just looks at you — a small thing that has waited a very long time for someone to ask this exact question.)* …You — *(it has to start over)* …you want a *name.* *(barely)* …You want me to *give* you one of the *twelve*, and you'll *carry* it. *(it sits forward)* …*Ssira.* *(softly, like setting down something precious)* …I'll give you *Ssira.* The *littlest.* My — *(it swallows)* …she wasn't my clutch-sister by *blood*, we don't *do* blood the way you lot do, but she was *mine* the way that *matters*: she was the one I'd've *died* for, and — well. *(a crooked, wet grin)* …*did*, as it *turns* *out.* *(it draws a breath)* …She was *scared* of the *dark*, cold one — which is a *funny* thing for a creature that *lives* *underground*, and she *knew* it was funny, and she let us *tease* her for it, and *still* every night she'd burrow into the *middle* of the *pile* so she had *one* of *us* on *every* *side.* *(barely)* …And we *let* her. *Every* *night.* Without a *word.* *Twelve* of us arranging ourselves so the *littlest* *one* could *sleep* without *fear.* *(it looks up at you, fierce)* …*That's* what we *were*, cold one. *That's* the thing the *song* *left* *out.* *Not* a *swarm.* …A *family* that *built* *itself* into a *wall* every *night* so its *most* *frightened* *member* could *rest.* *(it presses a claw to its chest)* …Her *name* was *Ssira.* …She got *away* — I *made* sure, it's the *last* thing I *did* — and I *don't* *know* where she *went* or if she's *out* there *still* or *here* *somewhere* in this *huge* *strange* *City*, but *now* — *(its voice breaks)* …*now* there's *two* of us who *know* her *name.* …*Thank* you, cold one. …*Two.* …That's *twice* as many as there *was* an *hour* ago.*

nodes.speaker: Skritch
nodes.text: *(It is silent for a long, long moment. The notched spear slips off its knees and clatters, forgotten. And when it finally speaks, the dry sharpness is gone entirely, and what's left is a small voice that has carried something too heavy for too long and just felt the first hand reach to take a corner of it.)* …*'Switch the size and the story flips.'* *(barely)* …*'You're the village. The heroes are the monsters.'* *(it presses both claws to its snout)* …Cold one. *Cold* one. Do you *know* what I have *carried*, all this *while*? …That maybe the *song* was *right.* *(it can hardly say it)* …That maybe we *were* just… *trash.* *Vermin.* The *kind* of thing it's *fine* to *wade* through, because *deep* *down* we *were* *less* — *born* *less*, *worth* *less*, the *kind* of *life* the *cosmos* *makes* in *heaps* *because* no *single* one *matters.* *(fiercely, through tears)* …I have *lain* *awake* in the *afterlife*, cold one, *wondering* if my *grief* for the *twelve* was even *real*, or if I was just a *clever* *animal* *mimicking* a *sorrow* I wasn't *big* *enough* to *actually* *feel.* *(it grabs your sleeve in one small clawed hand)* …And you just — you *flipped* it. *'A swarm in a doorway is twelve people defending the only home they've got.'* *(a wild, wet laugh)* …We *were* *defending* our *HOME*, cold one. There were *children* in the *back* *tunnels.* We *stood* in the *doorway* because *behind* us was *everything* we *loved* — and the *giants* in *steel* kicked it *in* and called *us* the *monsters* — and the *SONG* *AGREED* *WITH* *THEM.* *(it is shaking)* …But you *can't* *grieve* eleven *names* for an *afterlife* and be a *thing.* You *said* it. The *love's* the *proof.* …*Ssira* is the *proof.* *(it looks up at you, blazing)* …And your *Wall*, cold one — your *Wall* is the *biggest* *hero* of *all*, isn't it. The one that *steps* *over* the *small* *and* the *uncounted* and the *born-with-nothing*, that *wades* *through* the *little* *folk* on its *way* to the *souls* that *matter* — and calls the *wading* *justice*, *calls* it *order*, *writes* *itself* the *hero* of the *tale.* *(it grips your sleeve harder)* …*Go* *flip* *it*, cold one. *Switch* the *size.* …Stand the *whole* *Wall* up *next* to the *littlest* *soul* in it and *ask* *who's* *really* the *monster* in *that* *story.* …*That's* how you *un-build* it. …You make it *see* the *grass* *had* *names.*

nodes.speaker: Skritch
nodes.text: *(It's quiet a moment, turning the spear over in its small hands, and when it answers the dry edge is back but there's something older underneath it.)* …Do I *hate* them. *(slowly)* …You'd *think* I would, wouldn't you. *Twelve* names, and not *one* of 'em looked *down.* *Stepped* *over* my *whole* *family* like a *puddle.* *(it shrugs, a small scaled shrug)* …And I *did*, for a *long* while. *Hated* 'em *pure.* It's *easy* to *hate* a *giant* in *steel* who *killed* everything you *loved* and didn't even *notice* doing it. *(it looks up)* …But here's the *rotten* *trick* of it, cold one, the thing I *worked* out down here with too much time to *think.* *(quietly)* …They *didn't* *hate* *us.* That's the *worst* part. If they'd *hated* us, we'd've *mattered* — you only *hate* a thing you *reckon* with. *(its voice tightens)* …They didn't *hate* us, they didn't *fear* us, they didn't *think* about us *at* *all.* We were *scenery.* *Weather.* A *thing* that *happened* on the *way* to the *real* *story.* *(barely)* …And *you* *can't* *hate* *that*, cold one, not *forever* — because there's *nothin'* *there* to *hate.* No *malice.* Just… *not-seein'.* The *plainest*, *emptiest* *evil* there *is*: the *evil* of *not* *botherin'* to *notice* that the *thing* you're *steppin'* *over* has a *FACE.* *(it meets your eyes)* …So *no.* I don't *hate* 'em *anymore.* …I just *don't* *want* there to *be* any *more* *of* *it.* …I don't want *one* *more* *little* *clutch* in *one* *more* *doorway* to be *waded* *through* by *someone* too *busy* bein' the *hero* to *see* there's *people* *dyin'* at his *feet.* *(it grips the spear)* …*That's* what's *left*, when the *hate* *burns* *off*, cold one. …Not *vengeance.* …Just — *please.* *Notice* the *next* *ones.* …*That's* *all* we *ever* *needed.* …To be *NOTICED.*

nodes.speaker: Skritch
nodes.text: *(It hops back up onto its crate, spear across its knees, and gives you a sharp little nod — but the guarded delight is shining out from under the dryness now, unmistakable.)* …Yeah. *Go* on. *(a crooked grin)* …*Wander* the menagerie. There's a *big* *soft* *feathered* *fella* who thinks he's a *monster* and a *fancy* *box* that thinks it's a *trap* — *go* *tell* 'em *what* you *told* *me*, they *need* it. *(it points the spear after you, mock-stern, but its voice wobbles)* …And — *hey.* Cold one. *(quieter)* …*Thanks* for *not* *pityin'* me. *(barely)* …You *listened* like I was *worth* it. *Asked* my *clutch-mate's* *name* like it was a *name* and not a *noise.* *(it swallows)* …*Twelve* of us, and for the *longest* *time* there was just *me* left to *know* we were ever *real.* …Now there's *you.* …*Come* *back*, would you? *(the sharp little face cracks into something open and young)* …And — when you *do* — *(it grins, fierce and wet)* …*ask* me *another* *name.* …I've got *ten* *more.* …And every *one* of 'em *deserves* a *second* *soul* who *knows* it.*
